failure_surface: Python interpreter throws SyntaxError due to tool-call markup (<tool_call> characters) being injected into executable code cells instead of valid Python syntax.
root_cause: The agent generated improperly formatted tool invocation calls, embedding XML-style tool markers directly into code execution contexts where they were interpreted as Python code. Multiple execution attempts all failed with identical 'invalid syntax' errors pointing to the <tool_call> character at line 1.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Agents must ensure that code submitted to Python interpreters contains only valid Python syntax, with no tool-call markup embedded in the executable payload. Tool calls should wrap complete, clean code blocks without mixing execution context boundaries.
evidence: All execution attempts returned: Cell In[1], line 1 \n <tool_call>\n ^ SyntaxError: invalid syntax
The solution field shows malformed tool call with empty function body:
Multiple retry attempts produced identical syntax error, indicating systematic tool-formatting issue not resolved by code changes
Later attempts created files with valid Python code (/tmp/solution.py) but execution still failed at interpreter level

memory_items.title: Tool Call Syntax Integrity
memory_items.lesson: Never embed tool-call markers or markup syntax inside code cells intended for Python interpretation
memory_items.generalization: Separate tool invocation wrapping from executable payload content; ensure the interpreter receives only syntactically valid language code

memory_items.title: Execution Verification Before Continuing
memory_items.lesson: Always verify successful execution completion before proceeding to subsequent operations
memory_items.generalization: Check execution status and capture any syntax/runtime errors immediately after invoking code execution tools

memory_items.title: Code Block Boundary Awareness
memory_items.lesson: Maintain clear boundaries between tool call structures and code content to prevent parsing conflicts
memory_items.generalization: When using structured tool calling systems, validate that the full execution pipeline respects the separation between control syntax and data payloads

edits.content:
## Tool Execution Hygiene

### Code Block Formatting
- Never embed tool-call markup (e.g., XML tags, special markers) within executable code cells
- Ensure Python interpreters receive only syntactically valid Python, wrapped cleanly by tool invocation syntax
- Verify execution completes successfully before assuming downstream operations will work

### Error Detection
- Catch SyntaxError indicators at line 1 caused by non-Python characters in code payload
- When execution fails repeatedly with identical parsing errors, re-examine tool call structure before modifying code logic
